A malicious user with access to an end-to-end encrypted files drop link was able to also drop files into other end-to-end encrypted folders of the share owner. Reading and modifying of other files was not possible.

### Patches

It is recommended that the End-to-End-Encryption app is upgraded to 1.15.4, 1.16.3, 1.17.1 or 1.18.1

### Workarounds

* No workaround available

### References

* [HackerOne](https://hackerone.com/reports/3304830)
* [PullRequest](https://github.com/nextcloud/end_to_end_encryption/pull/1395)

### For more information

If you have any questions or comments about this advisory:

* Create a post in [nextcloud/security-advisories](https://github.com/nextcloud/security-advisories/discussions)
* Customers: Open a support ticket at [portal.nextcloud.com](https://portal.nextcloud.com)", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-p3qw-7gwx-wg24", "html_url": title: Files drop share links for end-to-end encrypted folders allowed to drop files into other folders of the share owner
